rulings.Case Title: BOBBY JAMES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 26, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF BOBBY JAMES' MOTION To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction Plaintiff Bobby James' motion to reclassify this action as an unlimited jurisdiction proceeding is denied. This personal injury action arising out of an accident that occurred on October 18, 2015 was filed on August 2, 2016, and all of the evidence of Plaintiff's medical treatment dates to 2015 and 2016. At Plaintiff's deposition, which was taken on December 15, 2016, Plaintiff testified that he was suffering residual pain from the accident. Plaintiff has failed to show good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier. (Code Civ. Proc. § 403.040(b)(2).) That counsel delayed for more than two years until shortly before the rescheduled arbitration in January 2019 before undertaking to fully evaluate the case, despite the earlier availability of medical records and other information that would have enabled such an evaluation, does not constitute such good cause. rulings.Case Title: COLIN HEILBUT VS. EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc. For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 26, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANTs EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. and EQUINOX HOLDINGS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Causes of action 1, 3, 5, and 9 all fail because Equinox is not vicariously liable for the alleged sexual assault as a matter of law. Equinox has a policy against sexual assault and such conduct is not part of normal yoga instruction. (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 2.) The fact that it is foreseeable that yoga instruction can involve touching does not create liability. (Lisa M. v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (1995) 12 Cal.4th 291, 302 ["In our view, that a job involves physical contact is, by itself, an insufficient basis on which to impose vicarious liability for a sexual assault."].) Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact that Equinox ratified the sexual assault. Mr. Nguyen was terminated 6 days after Plaintiff lodged his complaint. (Cf. Delfino v. Agilent Technologies, Inc. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 790, 811 [affirming summary judgment for employer which placed employee suspected of making online threats on administrative leave immediately after interview and terminated him eight days later].) Moreover, it is Plaintiff's position that Equinox "terminated Defendant Nguyen's employment for gross misconduct because it found [Plaintiff's] claim of sexual assault plausible and feared liability." (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 42.) This assertion undermines Plaintiff's ratification theory. Cause of action 2 fails because under the weight of recent authority, the Unruh Civil Rights Act "does not encompass discrimination based on retaliation." (Gayer v. Polk Gulch, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 515, 519-525 [claim that defendant bar excluded plaintiff in retaliation for his having previously filed a discrimination suit against its owner failed to state a cause of action under the Unruh Act]; see also Scripps Clinic v. Superior Court (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 917, 931-35 [finding that defendant medical group's policy of terminating medical care for patients who file lawsuits against its physicians did not constitute retaliatory discrimination that the Unruh Act was meant to preclude, as the policy "does not rely upon a patient's personal characteristics, but upon that patient's conduct"]; Moralez v. Whole Foods Market California, Inc. (N.D. Cal. Dec. 9, 2015) 2015 WL 8292079 at *4 [even assuming that plaintiff was subjected to disparate treatment, through retaliation, because she had litigated a prior civil rights case against defendant in the past, this does not give rise to an offense under the Unruh Act].) Cause of action 6 fails because the undisputed evidence establishes that Mr. Nguyen had no prior history of committing a sexual assault and Equinox had no notice of such a history. (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 4.) Plaintiff does not address this cause of action in his opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Equinox is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KERI FINDLEY VS. CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Certain Requests For Relief From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 26, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC, and KATHLEEN UTECHT's Motion To Strike Certain Requests For Relief From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint Defendants Core Venture Capital LLC and Kathleen Utecht's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Keri Findley's first amended complaint is denied as to the request for punitive damages and sustained with leave to amend as to the request for injunctive relief. Ms. Findley has sufficiently alleged that defendants engaged in malicious conduct with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights of others by alleging that defendants caused her termination in order to continue their illicit scheme. Ms. Fridley has not alleged ongoing conduct by Defendants or any threat of continuing misconduct. (See Madrid v. Perot Systems Corp. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 440, 465 ["Injunctive relief has no application to wrongs which have been completed, absent a showing that past violations will probably recur."].) Plaintiff is granted 20 days to amend to allege such ongoing misconduct if she can do so in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KERI FINDLEY VS. CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 26, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANTS CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC, AND KATHLEEN UTECHT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (Part 1 of 2 tentative ruling) Defendants Core Venture Capital LLC and Kathleen Utecht's demurrer to plaintiff Keri Findley's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for tortious interference with contractual relations, is overruled as to the second cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations, and is sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action for negligent interference with prospective economic advantage. The gist of Ms. Findley's first cause of action for tortious interference with contractual relations claim is that Defendants interfered with her contract to serve as a member of the Board of Directors of Solar Mosaic, Inc., a Delaware corporation, by removing her as a director. She alleges that Mosaic agreed to pay her $50,000 in cash, $100,000 in stock, and additional stock options annually as compensation for her services as a director. She contends that this contract was memorialized in two documents: an October 20, 2017 letter agreement entitled "Appointment to Board and Compensation as Director" (FAC, Ex. 1), and a Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement with Mosaic dated November 1, 2017 of which Defendants have sought judicial notice without opposition by Plaintiff. However, as both documents make clear, Plaintiff was elected to Mosaic's board by its shareholders. Under Delaware law, a director may be removed without or without cause by a majority of the stockholders. (8 Del. C. § 141(k).) A director, who has "actual or implied knowledge that [her] right to hold office may be extinguished" by the stockholders, therefore has no vested right in serving out a full term. (Roven v. Cotter (Del. Ch. 1988) 547 A.2d 603, 608, citing Everett v. Transnation Development Corp. (Del. Ch. 1970) 267 A.2d 627, 629-630.) Here, the letter agreement referred to Plaintiff's election by the stockholders to serve on the Board of Directors, and the Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement made Plaintiff's entitlement to receive shares "subject to [her] continued service as a director of the Company" and stated, "Nothing in this Agreement shall confer upon the Purchaser any right to continue in the Continued Service or interfere in any way with any right of the Company to terminate the Continuous Service at any time." Because Plaintiff does not allege that Defendants interfered in any way with the Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement or with her right to receive director fees, other than by removing her as a director of Mosaic, the demurrer to this claim is sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Findley has sufficiently alleged that Defendants engaged in independently wrongful conduct for the purposes of her second cause of action for intentional interference with economic advantage. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint alleges that defendants engaged in an illegal market manipulation scheme in breach of Ms. Utecht's fiduciary duty to a third party, and that they caused Ms. Findley's termination as a director in order to undermine her credibility and thereby prevent disruption of the scheme through public disclosure. Persons or entities with an ownership interest in a corporation are not automatically immune from liability for interfering with their corporation's contractual obligations. (See Popescu v. Apple Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 39, 52-54, citing Woods v. Fox Broadcasting Sub., Inc. (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 344, 353 [majority shareholder was not immune from tort liability for tortious interference with plaintiff employees' right to stock options].) TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUE ON PART 2 OF 2 =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FABRICOR LLC ET AL VS. GUNEET BAJWA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 26, 2019, Line 14, PLAINTIFFs FABRICOR LLC, and SAMUEL LEIZOREK's Motion And Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment Plaintiffs Fabricor LLC and Samuel Leizorek's application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment is granted in the amount of $1,081,315. Plaintiffs have carried their burden to show by a preponderance of the evidence (1) that the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) Plaintiffs have established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based; and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. (Code Civ. Proc. § 484.090(a); Santa Clara Waste Water Co. v. Allied World National Assurance Co. (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 881, 885 [$2.5 million writs of attachment affirmed where party seeking attachment established the probable validity of its claims]; United Central Bank v. Superior Court (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 212, 215 ["Case law holds that a writ of attachment may issue on a guaranty . . . ."]; Goldstein v. Barak Construction (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 845, 852.) As to the probable validity of Plaintiffs' claim, Defendants do not dispute that they executed the Guaranty by which they personally guaranteed payment to Plaintiffs of $1,055,480; that Plaintiffs made demand for payment of that amount; and that they have failed to pay that debt. Defendants' obligations under the Guaranty were not modified or released by any purported set-off or counterclaim which may be available to PresidioTCG or any other person or entity. Neither any alleged oral agreement nor the parties' subsequent course of conduct could have modified Defendants' obligations under the Guaranty, as by its terms that agreement may be amended only by a writing signed by the party or parties sought to be charged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
